{"command":"git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
add Haiku vs stronger-model calibration sampling per batch (#48)

Methodology currently names Haiku's lower reasoning ceiling as a
qualitative caveat in findings.md — a claim, not a measurement. This
adds the infrastructure to convert the claim to per-batch data.

- `next-batch` deterministically flags ~percent% of cells with

{"file_path":"/tmp/pr-body-48.md","content":"Closes #48

## Summary

Adds calibration-sampling infrastructure: per-batch ~percent% of cells get re-dispatched on a stronger model (Sonnet/Opus) and diffed against the Haiku transcript on the structured fields that drive every downstream metric. Converts "Haiku reasoning ceiling" from a qualitative caveat in `findings.md` into a per-batch number the Phase 5 analyst can surface.
